title: Insufficient Input Validation and Code Injection
description: Learn how poor input validation in database queries can allow attackers to inject malicious code and manipulate sensitive data.

attack_stages.title: Input Received
attack_stages.description: The system receives user input that is used in database queries.

attack_stages.title: Weak Input Validation
attack_stages.description: User input is not properly validated or sanitized before being used in database operations.

attack_stages.title: Query Manipulation
attack_stages.description: Malicious input alters the structure of a database query.

attack_stages.title: Database Exploitation
attack_stages.description: Attackers gain unauthorized access to or modify database records.

attack_stages.title: Business Impact
attack_stages.description: Sensitive data is exposed, modified, or deleted.

prevention_points.title: Strict Input Validation
prevention_points.description: Validate all input based on type, format, length, and allowed values before processing.

prevention_points.title: Parameterized Queries
prevention_points.description: Use prepared statements to ensure input is treated as data, not executable code.

prevention_points.title: Least Privilege Database Access
prevention_points.description: Limit database permissions to reduce the impact of compromised queries.

prevention_points.title: Web Application Firewall (WAF)
prevention_points.description: Detect and block known injection patterns before they reach backend systems.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A company database system allows employees to search, retrieve, and update records using input fields that are directly connected to backend queries.

nodes.title: How the System Works
nodes.text: The database system takes user input and uses it to build queries that retrieve or modify stored information. This input directly influences database operations.

nodes.title: Weak Input Validation
nodes.text: When user input is not properly validated or sanitized, attackers can insert malicious content into database queries. This allows them to manipulate how the database interprets requests.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to manipulate database queries to access, modify, or delete sensitive records without authorization.

nodes.title: What is the main risk of weak input validation?

choices.text: Malicious input may alter database queries

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Weak input validation allows attackers to manipulate how queries are executed. Think about how input affects database behavior.

nodes.title: Query Manipulation
nodes.text: Because input is not properly sanitized, the attacker is able to manipulate database queries and change how the system retrieves or modifies data.

nodes.title: Database Exploitation
nodes.text: The attacker can now access or modify sensitive records stored in the database, bypassing normal access controls.

nodes.title: Business Impact
nodes.text: Improper input validation can lead to unauthorized data access, modification, deletion of records, and loss of database integrity.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should validate all input strictly, use parameterized queries, enforce least privilege database access, and deploy monitoring tools to detect abnormal query behavior.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how unsafe input handling allowed an attacker to manipulate database queries and access sensitive information.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Weak input validation in database-driven systems can allow attackers to manipulate queries and compromise sensitive data. Proper validation and query separation are essential defenses.
